name: Browser Control
summary: Drive ANY browser (Firefox/Chrome/Chromium/Brave/Edge/…) through browser:// URI commands — by KVM GUI control (launch + keyboard/mouse/screenshot/OCR-click via tellmesh urihim/urikvm), by headless Chrome (read/screenshot), or forwarded to a noVNC/urirun node.
description: Browser Control exposes browser actions as URI commands across three targets. browser://kvm/... drives ANY visible browser by GUI — launch a chosen browser, navigate (Ctrl+L → type → Enter), type, hotkey, click coordinates, OCR-click an on-screen label, capture the screen, close — implemented by reusing the tellmesh modules (urihim keyboard/mouse, urikvm screenshot/OCR) as the handlers, so the same surface controls every browser, its extensions, logins and plugins. browser://chrome/... drives a local headless Chrome/Chromium for DOM/text/screenshot (safe dry-run if absent). browser://desktop/... forwards to a noVNC/urirun node (BROWSER_CONTROL_ENDPOINT / URI_SERVICE_MAP). KVM routes need a desktop session (DISPLAY/WAYLAND) and the tellmesh packs (or TELLMESH_DIR); they return a clean error otherwise.
useCases: Control any browser (Firefox/Chrome/…) by GUI over URI — login, fill forms, click by visible label, screenshot.
Drive a remote desktop's browser via KVM from NL-to-URI workflows (no per-browser API).
Read a page / capture a screenshot headlessly for QA artifacts.
requires: desktop session (DISPLAY/WAYLAND) + tellmesh him/kvm for browser://kvm; or headless Chrome; or a noVNC/urirun node
